description: Measures retail sales levels and momentum as a direct read on consumer demand.
why_classified: Retail sales are a core component of consumption and GDP; changes signal demand strength and can feed into goods inflation.
author: Evan

description: Survey of consumer attitudes that tends to lead spending behaviour.
why_classified: Sentiment surveys capture forward consumer intentions and can anticipate changes in consumption; they also reflect sentiment risk which can influence markets.
author: Evan

description: Equity index tracking semiconductor sector performance and cyclicality.
why_classified: Semiconductor equities are cyclically sensitive and correlate with tech capex and demand, making them a sector-level activity proxy.
author: Evan

description: Purchasing managers' survey for manufacturing activity and input prices.
why_classified: PMI components reflect production, new orders and input cost pressures—timely indicators of real activity and inflation.
author: Evan

description: Survey of services-sector activity, backlog and prices.
why_classified: Services PMI captures demand and price pressures in services—important for modern economies where services dominate GDP.
author: Evan

description: Ratio of gold to copper prices highlighting safe-haven vs cyclical commodity dynamics.
why_classified: Gold often reflects safety demand while copper tracks industrial demand—their ratio signals balance between safe-haven and cyclical forces.
author: Evan

description: Relative pricing between gold and silver, often used as a liquidity/safety proxy.
why_classified: Shifts in precious metals relative valuation can reflect market risk appetite and liquidity preference.
author: Evan

description: Monthly measure of new single-family home sales—direct demand indicator for housing.
why_classified: New home sales directly reflect buyer demand and are sensitive to rates and consumer confidence.
author: Evan

description: Authorisations for new residential construction—forward-looking supply metric.
why_classified: Permits lead starts and construction activity, indicating pipeline supply and future employment in construction.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Producer Price Index by industry: Semiconductor and Other Electronic Component Manufacturing
description: PPI for semiconductor component manufacturing capturing input price trends in tech supply chain.
why_classified: Sector PPI highlights goods price pressures that can propagate to margins and consumer prices in electronics.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Producer Price Index by Industry: Electronic Computer Manufacturing
description: PPI for computer manufacturing reflecting goods price movements in IT hardware.
why_classified: Tracks price pressure in a key durable goods sector that affects broader manufacturing inflation readings.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Producer Price Index by Commodity: Professional Services (Partial): Information Technology (IT) Technical Support and Consulting Services
description: PPI component measuring price changes for IT technical support and consulting services.
why_classified: Services PPI for IT captures input costs and pricing power in the tech services segment.
author: Evan

description: Implied volatility index for the S&P 500—market fear gauge.
why_classified: VIX reflects option-implied expected volatility and is a leading indicator of market stress and hedging demand.
author: Evan

description: Spread between interbank lending and short-term safe rates indicating interbank risk.
why_classified: Widening TED spread signals counterparty risk or liquidity stress in the banking system.
author: Evan

description: Spread between SOFR and 3M reference showing short-term funding/policy dynamics.
why_classified: This spread captures tightness in funding markets relative to policy rates and can signal liquidity strains.
author: Evan

description: Yield curve slope between 10-year and 2-year Treasuries—classic recession/expectations signal.
why_classified: Curve inversion and slope changes reflect growth expectations and term premium shifts important for macro outlooks.
author: Evan

description: Aggregate short interest within sector ETFs as a proxy for market positioning.
why_classified: High short interest indicates concentrated negative positioning, which can precede squeezes or signal stress.
author: Evan

description: Measures industrial output across manufacturing, mining and utilities.
why_classified: Direct measure of real economy production and cyclical activity.
author: Evan

description: Monetary aggregate capturing cash, deposits and near-money—signal of liquidity conditions.
why_classified: Changes in M2 influence aggregate demand and can preface inflationary or deflationary trends.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Expected Change in Business Conditions in a Year (Table 26)
description: Survey-based expected change in business conditions over the coming year.
why_classified: Forward-looking expectations often translate into investment and hiring decisions, signaling future activity.
author: Evan

description: Percent of the labor force unemployed—core labor market indicator.
why_classified: Labor market slack influences wage pressure and demand, central to growth and inflation dynamics.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Producer Price Index > Final demand less foods and energy - WPUFD49104
description: Core PPI excluding volatile food and energy components—measures underlying PPI inflation.
why_classified: Provides a cleaner signal of producer price trends that feed into consumer inflation.
author: Evan

description: Measures changes in labor compensation, a key input to unit labor costs.
why_classified: Wage growth is a primary driver of services inflation and a forward input to price pressures.
author: Evan

description: Prices paid for imports—can transmit to domestic inflation via pass-through.
why_classified: Import price shifts reflect global commodity and goods price changes that affect domestic PCE/PPI.
author: Evan

description: Ratio of inventories to sales at retailers—signal of demand weakness or excess supply.
why_classified: Rising inventories relative to sales can precede demand slowdowns and markdown pressure.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Manufacturers' New Orders: Nondefense Capital Goods Excluding Aircraft (NEWORDER)
description: New orders for business investment goods excluding aircraft—proxy for corporate capex demand.
why_classified: Reflects investment appetite and can foreshadow manufacturing output and equipment orders.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Loans and Leases in Bank Credit, All Commercial Banks
description: Aggregate bank lending outstanding—indicator of credit supply and financial cycle.
why_classified: Credit expansion or contraction signals risk appetite and funding availability for firms and households.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Producer Price Index by Industry: Oil and Gas Field Machinery and Equipment Manufacturing
description: PPI for oilfield machinery—reflects cost pressures in energy-related capital goods.
why_classified: Important for assessing input cost trends in energy sector capex and drilling activity.
author: Evan

description: Rate of credit card defaults—direct signal of household financial stress.
why_classified: Rising defaults indicate consumer balance-sheet deterioration and can presage consumption weakness.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Annual Rate for Housing Units Authorized in Permit-Issuing Places
description: Annualized rate of housing permits—forward indicator of construction supply.
why_classified: Permits reflect builders' plans and signal future construction employment and materials demand.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Annual Rate for Housing Units Started
description: Annualized housing starts rate—real-time measure of construction commencement.
why_classified: Starts indicate immediate construction activity and employment needs in the housing sector.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Annual Rate for New Single-Family Houses Sold
description: Annualized sales rate for new single-family homes—demand-side housing metric.
why_classified: Direct measure of buyer activity in new housing, sensitive to rates and sentiment.
author: Evan

description: Index tracking momentum in nonresidential construction projects.
why_classified: Construction project momentum anticipates future construction spending and employment in nonresidential sectors.
author: Evan

description: Architect billings as a leading indicator for nonresidential construction activity.
why_classified: Billing trends precede actual construction starts and reflect business investment demand.
author: Evan

indicator_name: ICE BofA US High Yield Index Option-Adjusted Spread
description: Option-adjusted spread for high-yield bonds indicating credit risk premia.
why_classified: HY spreads widen in stress and reflect lenders' required compensation for credit risk; central credit stress barometer.
author: Evan

description: Freight shipments index reflecting goods movement and trade activity.
why_classified: Freight volumes closely track goods demand and industrial activity, useful for cyclical assessment.
author: Evan

description: Employment in transportation and warehousing sectors—indicator of goods movement labor demand.
why_classified: Labor trends in logistics are sensitive to trade volumes and supply-chain activity, reflecting real activity.
author: Evan

description: After-tax personal income available for spending—supports consumption.
why_classified: Disposable income is a primary determinant of consumer spending ability and thus aggregate demand.
author: Evan

indicator_name: U.S. Exports of Services: Telecommunications, Computer, and Information Services
description: Exports of ICT services—signal of foreign demand for tech services.
why_classified: Export strength in services reflects external demand and sectoral competitiveness in tech services.
author: Evan

description: PCE component for services capturing consumption and price contribution from services.
why_classified: Services PCE is a major share of consumption and a key driver of service-sector inflation.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Net Percentage of Domestic Banks Tightening Standards for Commercial and Industrial Loans to Large and Middle-Market Firms
description: Net percent of banks reporting tighter lending standards for C&I loans—early credit cycle indicator.
why_classified: Net tightening signals reduced credit availability which can slow investment and amplify stress in downturns.
author: Evan

description: Difference between HY and IG spreads capturing risk dispersion across credit tiers.
why_classified: Widening HY-IG spread indicates greater stress in lower-rated credits relative to investment grade, signaling risk-off dynamics.
author: Evan

description: Total electricity generated across sectors—proxy for industrial and economic activity.
why_classified: Electricity generation is closely tied to industrial output and overall energy demand, a real activity indicator.
author: Evan

description: Total electricity consumption reflecting end‑use demand patterns.
why_classified: Consumption captures real economic activity in households and industry; changes signal demand shifts.
author: Evan

description: Imports of natural gas into the U.S.—indicator of domestic supply/demand balance and trade flows.
why_classified: Import/export flows reflect global demand and domestic production dynamics that affect energy prices.
author: Evan

description: Exports of natural gas—indicator of external demand and domestic surplus dynamics.
why_classified: Growing exports can signal global demand strength and domestic production surplus impacting prices.
author: Evan

description: PCE component for nondurable goods measuring consumption and price trends for consumables.
why_classified: Tracks short‑lived goods demand and goods price pressures which affect headline PCE dynamics.
author: Evan

description: PCE contribution from housing‑related consumption and services prices.
why_classified: Housing-related consumption and rental equivalent prices are major contributors to services inflation.
author: Evan

description: PCE component for health-care services and goods—tracks demand and price changes in health.
why_classified: Health is a large, structural services category with distinct inflation dynamics and low frequency movements.
author: Evan

description: PCE component tracking prices for financial services and insurance.
why_classified: Sectoral services price signal; lower frequency and limited direct impact on core inflation decisions.
author: Evan

description: PCE component for communication services measuring price and consumption.
why_classified: Captures prices in telecom/communication services; sector-specific pricing dynamics.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Health System Tracker - National Health Spending Explorer
description: Dataset outlining national health spending over time—contextual, low-frequency.
why_classified: Provides structural, long-run perspective on health expenditures rather than high-frequency forecasting value.
author: Evan

description: International dataset for health expenditure comparisons—contextual reference.
why_classified: Useful for structural cross-country analysis; low frequency and not a short-term trading signal.
author: Evan

description: Inventory levels for petroleum and liquids—indicator of near-term supply pressure.
why_classified: Inventory draws or builds directly influence crude price dynamics and near-term supply tightness.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Crude Oil Prices: West Texas Intermediate (WTI)
description: Benchmark crude oil price—direct input to energy inflation and economic shocks.
why_classified: WTI price moves have immediate macroeconomic and inflationary implications for energy-intensive sectors.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Refinery Utilization & Capacity - Operable Utilization Rate
description: Refinery utilization rate measuring capacity use and potential supply bottlenecks.
why_classified: High utilization signals tight refining capacity which can amplify crude price moves and product shortages.
author: Evan

description: Aggregate primary energy consumption across fuels—low-frequency activity proxy.
why_classified: Broad energy demand trends map to long-run economic activity; low-frequency and structural.
author: Evan

description: Total electricity generation across sectors—contextual activity measure.
why_classified: Useful as a long-run activity proxy but low-frequency; less useful for short-term trading signals.
author: Evan

description: Breakdown of electricity end-use across sectors—structural demand information.
why_classified: Provides sectoral demand structure useful for long-term analysis rather than high-frequency timing.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Pharmaceutical and Other Medical Products Expenditures Price Index
description: Price index for pharmaceutical and medical product expenditures.
why_classified: Tracks sectoral price pressure in pharmaceuticals, relevant for health sector inflation analysis.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Health Expenditures Price Index, MEPS Account Basis
description: Health expenditure price index on a MEPS basis—sectoral price measure.
why_classified: Useful to track health-sector inflation dynamics which can be persistent and structural.
author: Evan

description: Year-over-year change in electricity prices—direct energy inflation signal.
why_classified: Provides direct evidence of energy price inflation affecting consumer and producer prices.
author: Evan

description: Comparison of electricity YoY change versus CPI YoY to show relative contribution.
why_classified: Helps isolate electricity's share of headline CPI movements and contribution to inflation.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Average Price of Electricity to Ultimate Customers (YoY)
description: Average end-customer electricity price YoY—consumer-facing energy inflation signal.
why_classified: Directly measures the consumer price experience for electricity which feeds into CPI components.
author: Evan

description: Cement shipments as a proxy for construction activity and materials demand.
why_classified: Construction materials shipments are leading indicators for building and infrastructure activity.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Producer Price Index by Industry: Telecommunications
description: PPI for telecommunications industry—sectoral input price measure.
why_classified: Sector PPI signals input cost movements and potential pass-through to consumer prices in telecom.
author: Evan

description: Count of novel drug approvals—a long-lead indicator for pharma sector innovation.
why_classified: Approval counts reflect innovation pipeline health but are low-frequency and not predictive for short-term markets.
author: Evan

description: Weekly new unemployment claims—timely indicator of labor market deterioration.
why_classified: Spikes in initial claims are a rapid signal of labor market stress and can precede consumption weakness.
author: Evan

description: Ongoing insured unemployment continuing claims—measures persistent labor-market slack.
why_classified: Continued claims indicate the depth and persistence of unemployment beyond weekly shocks.
author: Evan

description: Total job vacancies signaling hiring demand and labor market tightness (JOLTS).
why_classified: High openings indicate demand for labor and wage pressure potential; low openings signal cooling.
author: Evan

description: ADP private payroll change—timely indicator of employment trends in the private sector.
why_classified: Provides higher-frequency private-sector employment reads, useful as a cross-check to official payrolls.
author: Evan

description: Regional diffusion index for manufacturing activity—localised activity signal.
why_classified: Regional Fed surveys capture local manufacturing conditions that can presage national trends.
author: Evan

description: Personal Consumption Expenditures headline price index—central policy inflation measure.
why_classified: PCE is the Fed's preferred inflation gauge and directly influences policy decisions.
author: Evan

description: Core PCE excluding food and energy—focus for policy assessment of underlying inflation.
why_classified: Removes volatile components to highlight persistent inflation trends that guide policy.
author: Evan

description: Price component within the ISM Services PMI capturing services-sector price pressures.
why_classified: Direct survey evidence of services price pressures and cost pass-through expectations.
author: Evan

description: Builder confidence index for single-family housing demand—leading housing sentiment indicator.
why_classified: Builder sentiment anticipates construction activity and housing starts, sensitive to rates and demand.
author: Evan

description: Spread between 10-year and 3-month Treasuries—alternate term spread and recession signal.
why_classified: Variant of the term structure that captures near-term policy expectations and term premium dynamics.
author: Evan

description: Ratio of 1‑month to 3‑month VIX indicating near-term vs term implied volatility dynamics.
why_classified: Changes in the vol term structure reflect shifts in near-term risk expectations and term risk.
author: Evan

description: Blended measure of health expenditure prices—low-frequency sectoral inflation metric.
why_classified: Aggregates health price measures to provide a composite view; low-frequency relevance.
author: Evan

indicator_name: Health Services Expenditures Price Index, MEPS
description: MEPS-based health services price index—tracks prices in health services.
why_classified: Specific measure for health services inflation useful for sectoral analysis.
author: Evan
